data.narrative:
Last Energy, a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Bret Kugelmass, has raised $140M+ to date. The broader nuclear energy sector comp universe includes 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 7 private peers including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Tokamak Energy.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $140M total raised as a valuation proxy, implying a post-money valuation of $238M. This multiplier reflects typical Series C to subsequent-stage step-ups observed in venture progression. Peer-median comparison was not applicable; while 4 same-sector nuclear peers exist, none satisfied the threshold of three or more comparables at identical or adjacent stages with similar valuation tiers. A broader all-sector median would introduce undue noise given nuclear's concentrated and nascent investor base.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $202M (low) to $274M (high), with a median of $238M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the stage-progression multiplier for frontier-stage nuclear, the absence of tight peer benchmarks in this nascent subsector, and whether $140M in cumulative raises accurately reflects current valuation relative to deployment and technical milestone progression.
